reason: The passage explicitly contains God's declaration of an upcoming supernatural judgment upon the entire Israelite congregation. God announces through Moses that all adults (those 20 years and older) will fall dead in the wilderness (Numbers 14:29: 'בַּמִּדְבָּ֣ר הַ֠זֶּה יִפְּל֨וּ פִגְרֵיכֶ֜ם' - 'in this wilderness your bodies will fall'). This is not an individual destruction but a mass catastrophic event affecting the general public - specifically, an entire generation of Israel will perish over 40 years as divine punishment for their rebellion and faithlessness. Additionally, God declares a plague ('בַדֶּ֖בֶר' - 'with pestilence') that immediately strikes down the ten spies who brought the bad report (Numbers 14:37). The destruction is clearly supernatural in origin, ordained by Yahweh himself in response to Israel's rejection of His promise.
